label: BackgroundWorker
documentation: Utility class for executing tasks asynchronously in background threads.

label: submit
documentation: Submit a lambda task to the background thread. Note: Exception handling (try/catch) for the task should be done within the lambda function.

label: SoundManager
documentation: Manager for playing static or dynamic sounds in the game environment.

label: playLocalSound
detail: SoundManager.playLocalSound(id: Identifier, volume: float, pitch: float): void
documentation: Plays a sound that is NOT localized to a specific position; played at a constant volume. Ideal for UI or train announcements.

label: playSound
detail: SoundManager.playSound(id: Identifier, pos: Vector3f, volume: float, pitch: float): void
documentation: Plays a sound at a specific `Vector3f` position in the world relative to the current base position.

label: play
documentation: Begins playing a `TickableSoundInstance` in the game.

label: TickableSoundInstance
documentation: Represents a sound that can be modified (position, volume, pitch) or looped during playback.

label: create
documentation: Creates a new sound instance. `soundCategory` values: `MASTER`, `BLOCKS`, `MUSIC`, `RECORDS`, `WEATHER`, `HOSTILE`, `NEUTRAL`, `PLAYERS`, `AMBIENT`, `VOICE`.

label: setSoundVolume
documentation: Sets the volume of the sound instance. `1.0` is default.

label: setSoundPitch
documentation: Sets the pitch of the sound instance. `1.0` is default.

label: setLoopable
documentation: Sets whether the sound should automatically restart after finishing.

label: setLoopDelay
documentation: Sets the delay in Minecraft Ticks before a loopable sound repeats.

label: setRelative
documentation: If `true`, the sound follows the player at a static volume. If `false`, it uses the world position set by `setPos()`.

label: setPos
documentation: Sets the 3D position of the sound instance.

label: isPlaying
documentation: Returns true if the sound is currently active/playing.
